title: Convert Speed to $$\frac{m}{s}$$
text: First, convert the initial speed from $$\frac{km}{h}$$ to $$\frac{m}{s}$$. Remember that $$1$$ $$\frac{km}{h}$$ is equal to $$\frac{1}{3.6}$$ $$\frac{m}{s}$$.

title: Calculate Initial Speed in $$\frac{m}{s}$$
text: Use the conversion factor to find the initial speed in $$\frac{m}{s}$$. What is $$1000$$ $$\frac{km}{h}$$ in $$\frac{m}{s}$$?

title: Initial Speed in $$\frac{m}{s}$$
text: Calculate the initial speed in $$\frac{m}{s}$$ using the conversion factor.

title: Use the Formula for Acceleration
text: The formula for acceleration is $$a=\frac{v_f-v_i}{t}$$, where $$v_f$$ is the final velocity, $$v_i$$ is the initial velocity, and $$t$$ is the time.

title: Calculate Acceleration
text: Substitute the values into the formula: $$v_f=0$$ $$\frac{m}{s}$$, $$v_i=277.78$$ $$\frac{m}{s}$$, and $$t=1.10$$ s.
